issues.description: Questions reference non-existent text passages and specific paragraphs without providing the actual reading passage
issues.suggestion: Include the complete reading passage that students will analyze, or make questions generic enough to work with any text

issues.description: Multiple choice questions are impossible to answer without the referenced text (e.g., 'paragraph two', 'paragraph three')
issues.suggestion: Either provide the text or rework questions to be text-independent examples

issues.description: Questions 9-10 may be too advanced for grade 6, requiring analysis and evaluation skills typical of higher grades
issues.suggestion: Simplify questions 9-10 to focus more on identification and basic inference rather than complex analysis

issues.description: While questions address citing evidence, they lack focus on explicit vs. implicit information distinction emphasized in RI.6.1
issues.suggestion: Add questions that specifically distinguish between what the text states directly versus what can be inferred

issues.description: Answer key explanations for short response questions are too generic and lack specific criteria or rubrics
issues.suggestion: Provide more detailed rubrics or sample responses for questions 6-10
